rulings.Case Title: DONALD NORMAN VS. PATRICK STRATEMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Objections To Evidence And Evidence In Support Of Reply To Motion To Deny Approval And Reply To Motion To Conduct Limited Discovery
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 4, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFF DONALD NORMAN IN THE RIGHT OF AND FOR THE BENEFIT OF INTERSANGO, LLC'S Motion To Seal Objections To Evidence And Evidence In Support Of Reply To Motion To Deny Approval And Reply To Motion To Conduct Limited Discovery. Hearing required. Assuming there is no objection to the untimely filing and service of the motion, the court's indicated appears below. The unopposed motion to seal is granted. The court has reviewed the material to be sealed and the papers in support of this motion and finds that the material to be sealed contains confidential financial information and other customer information. The redactions are narrowly tailored. The court finds that (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: G. S. VS. CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Information Filed In Support Of Plaitniff'S Opposition To California Physicians' Service Dba Blue Shield Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 4, 2026, line 10. PLAINTIFF G. S. Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Information Filed In Support Of Plaitniff'S Opposition To California Physicians' Service Dba Blue Shield Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff G.S.'s unopposed motion to seal is GRANTED. G.S. moves to seal Exhibits 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 11 to her appendix of evidence in opposition to Defendant Blue Shield's motion for summary judgment. The court finds that the private nature of the documents is apparent on their face. Exhibits 1, 2, 6, and 11 are designated as confidential pursuant to the protective order entered by this court on August 15, 2024. Exhibits 3, 4, and 5 discuss medical information and mental health care treatment given to G.S.'s then-minor daughter, S.S. As to all exhibits that G.S. moves to seal, the court finds that (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 4, 2026, line 5. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: 1000 VAN NESS LP VS. 5910 INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 4, 2026, line 6. PLAINTIFF 1000 VAN NESS LP'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Moving party 1000 Van Ness LP's motion to compel further responses to interrogatory numbers 13, 14, and 15, and to requests for production numbers 22, 23, and 24, is granted, but the court denies sanctions to both parties. Discovery is liberally granted, and the court concludes the requests and interrogatories at issue are relevant and proportional to the litigation. Responding party 5910 Investments, LLC's objections largely lack merit except as discussed below. The stay issue has been resolved in Department 501. 5910's objection based on the financial privacy of third parties has merit but can be resolved with a protective order. The court orders the parties to meet and confer within one week about a protective order. The court will continue the hearing on this motion to February 13, 2026 to ensure entry of the protective order. 5910's objection based on prior discovery responses lacks merit because the prior responses were not verified. 5910 shall respond to the discovery at issue in full within one week after the protective order is entered. The court "shall" grant sanctions to a party who is unsuccessful in opposing a motion for further responses to interrogatories or requests for production unless it finds that the opposing party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. Here, the court concludes that the stay motion, while unsuccessful, was colorable; although the Receiver stated an intent to stay out of the dispute raised by the discovery at issue here, 5910 presented efficiency and other arguments to the court in its stay motion that the better course was to delegate that responsibility to the receiver. The court therefore concludes that 5910's position had substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: SIDA WANG FAGGIOLI ET AL VS. JAGUAR LAND ROVER NORTH AMERICA, LLC, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Defendant'S Failure To Comply With C.C.P. Section 871.26, Subd. (B) Initial Disclosure And Document Exchange Requirement
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 4, 2026, line 8. PLAINTIFFS SIDA FAGGIOLI, NATHANIEL FAGGIOLI'S Motion For Sanctions For Defendant'S Failure To Comply With C.C.P. Section 871.26, Subd. (B) Initial Disclosure And Document Exchange Requirement. Plaintiffs' motion for sanctions is granted, not based on defendant Jaguar's failure to produce without a protective order but on its failure to substantiate its inability to produce based on a cyber-incident and its conditioning of production on subscription payments.
